user_query:
Hey! Can you prep a draft for our 2026-04-20 planning meeting using the materials in the input/ folder and save the outputs under output/? Here’s what I need:

Inputs (read and extract from all of these):
- input/agendas/next_meeting_agenda.md (contains the meeting title, date, and agenda with some [ACTION] items that have assignee and due dates)
- input/notes/last_meeting_notes.md (has checkbox-style action items: - [ ] for incomplete and - [x] for done)
- input/committees/volunteer_roster.csv (names, emails, and roles to validate assignees)
- input/committees/prior_commitments.json (tasks with assignee, due, and status)
- input/research/policy_brief.txt (campaign context with numeric facts)

Deliverables:
1) output/meeting_minutes_draft.md with the following sections:
   - Title line that includes the meeting name and the date from the agenda file (e.g., “Mutual Aid Transit Campaign — Planning Meeting (2026-04-20)”).
   - Agenda: include every agenda line from input/agendas/next_meeting_agenda.md in the same order, including any [ACTION] sub-bullets.
   - Context: include exactly the first three lines from input/research/policy_brief.txt that contain at least one digit (preserve the text of those lines).
   - Carryover Action Items (roster-matched): list items that are (a) status == "open" in input/committees/prior_commitments.json and (b) unchecked (- [ ]) in input/notes/last_meeting_notes.md, but only if the assignee name matches a row in input/committees/volunteer_roster.csv. For each item, include assignee name, role, email, task, due date, and source ("prior_commitments" or "last_meeting_notes").
   - New Action Items from Agenda (roster-matched): extract all [ACTION] lines from input/agendas/next_meeting_agenda.md and include them here only if the assignee appears in input/committees/volunteer_roster.csv. For each, include assignee name, role, email, task, due date, and source as "agenda".
   - Unassigned or Missing Contacts: list any remaining tasks from the three sources above where the assignee is NOT found in input/committees/volunteer_roster.csv. For each, include assignee, task, due date, and source.

2) output/action_items.csv containing ONLY roster-matched action items from all three sources above (open items from prior_commitments.json, unchecked items from last_meeting_notes.md, and [ACTION] items from the agenda). Use these columns exactly in this order: source,task,assignee,email,role,due_date,origin_file. Populate origin_file with the path to the file the item came from.

3) output/missing_contacts.json listing action items (grouped by assignee) for any tasks where the assignee does not appear in input/committees/volunteer_roster.csv, across the three sources. Structure it as an array of objects with: assignee (string) and items (array of {task, due_date, source, origin_file}).

Notes and constraints:
- Treat status == "done" in prior_commitments.json and checked (- [x]) items in last_meeting_notes.md as completed and exclude them from action items.
- For name matching, use exact string matches between assignee names and the "name" column in the roster CSV.
- Keep due dates as YYYY-MM-DD strings.
- The agenda date in output/meeting_minutes_draft.md must match the Date field in input/agendas/next_meeting_agenda.md.
- All three outputs should be reproducible from the inputs above without any external data.

Paths to produce:
- output/meeting_minutes_draft.md
- output/action_items.csv
- output/missing_contacts.json

Thanks! This will help us walk into the meeting with a clean draft and a consolidated action tracker we can all review. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
